mnemonics.title: Directory Types Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: SINGLE TWO HIERARCHY ACYCLIC GENERAL - STAGES IN ORDER: Single-Level, Two-Level, Hierarchical, Acyclic Graph, General Graph Directory structures.

mnemonics.title: Directory Operations
mnemonics.content: CRUD FOR DIRECTORIES: Create, Read (list), Update (rename/move), Delete - standard operations on directories.

mnemonics.title: Path Types
mnemonics.content: ABSOLUTE ALWAYS START WITH ROOT (/), RELATIVE ALWAYS START FROM CURRENT (./ or ../) - remember the starting point difference.

acronyms.usage: Top-level directory in two-level structure containing user entries
Private directory assigned to each user in two-level structure
Maintains current working directory information for each process
Mechanism for directory and file protection specifying user permissions
Data structure storing file metadata in directory entries

visualTips.visualization: Draw an inverted tree with root at top. Main branches are subdirectories, leaves are files. Use lines to show parent-child relationships.
Draw a ladder - root at top. Absolute path = start from top ladder rung every time. Relative path = start from your current rung.
Use arrows to show links pointing to shared nodes. Ensure NO circular paths - imagine water flowing, it should never loop back.
Draw boxes (buckets) with filenames as keys. Use consistent hash function to map names to buckets - like a dictionary with alphabet sections.

analogies.analogy: Just as a physical folder holds papers, a directory holds files. You can have folders within folders - same as subdirectories.
A person can have multiple nicknames - all refer to the same person. Hard links are like nicknames for files.
A desktop shortcut points to an application location. If you move the original, shortcut breaks. Symbolic links work similarly.

analogies.concept: Current Working Directory as Your Location
analogies.analogy: When someone asks 'where are you?', your answer is your current location. Relative paths work the same way - from where you currently are.
